name: Google Vertex AI
description: Google Cloud's enterprise model platform (Gemini + third-party model garden)

provider.comment: Operated by Google Cloud (Google LLC) as its enterprise AI/ML platform; rebranded 'Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform' in 2026 (checked 2026-07).

category.comment: Hyperscaler cloud platform serving many vendors' models (Gemini plus Anthropic, Meta, Mistral, AI21, Qwen, DeepSeek, and self-deployed open models) behind one API with IAM, regions, and compliance. Google's own first-party Gemini developer API (AI Studio) is tracked as a separate candidate; on Vertex it is one of many models on the platform.

api-launched.comment: Generative AI (LLM inference) support on Vertex AI reached general availability in June 2023 (previewed at Google I/O, May 2023). The broader Vertex AI ML platform itself went GA in May 2021; this date reflects the generative/LLM inference API, which is the scope here (checked 2026-07).

status.comment: Generative AI on Vertex AI is generally available with published commercial pricing; individual newest models may ship in preview.

api-docs.comment: Official Generative AI on Vertex AI documentation and API reference.

auth-methods.comment: Requests authenticate with Google Cloud IAM identities: service accounts and OAuth 2.0 access tokens via Application Default Credentials (the OpenAI-compatible endpoint passes a GCP access token as the api_key). Vertex AI 'Express mode' additionally issues a simple API key for quick starts (checked 2026-07).

self-serve-signup.comment: Self-serve via a Google Cloud account/project with immediate pay-as-you-go activation; no sales contact required. Requires having/creating a Google Cloud account (there is no standalone Vertex-only key outside GCP, though Express mode lowers that friction).

free-credits-trial.comment: Google Cloud free trial grants $300 in credits for 90 days, usable on Vertex AI; grounding with Google Search also includes a free daily/monthly query allowance for some Gemini models (checked 2026-07).

regional-endpoints.comment: Available across many Google Cloud regions in the US, Europe, and Asia Pacific; a 'global' endpoint with dynamic routing is also offered for maximum availability.

data-residency-options.comment: Customers can pin ML processing and storage to a chosen region/jurisdiction (regional endpoints plus data-residency and ML-processing-location controls), a contractual/config guarantee rather than merely multiple endpoints.

model-breadth.comment: Model Garden serves families from many vendors: Google (Gemini, Gemma), Anthropic (Claude), Meta (Llama), Mistral AI, AI21, Alibaba (Qwen), DeepSeek, MiniMax, and more.

open-weight-models.comment: Open-weight models available including Meta Llama, Google Gemma, Mistral, Qwen, and DeepSeek, with self-deployment via prebuilt or custom (vLLM) containers.

model-version-pinning.comment: Specific dated/stable model versions can be pinned (e.g. gemini-2.5-pro stable snapshots) rather than only a floating alias.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: Supports supervised tuning of Gemini plus deploying your own/open-weight models to Vertex AI endpoints (self-deployed models, custom containers), all callable through the same platform API.

model-catalog-size.comment: Model Garden lists well over 100 models (commonly cited as 200+) across first-party, partner, and open-weight self-deploy options; exact count varies and changes frequently (checked 2026-07).

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: Modern multi-turn messages via generateContent, plus an OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions endpoint and Anthropic Messages for Claude models.

streaming.comment: Token streaming via streamGenerateContent (SSE) and the bidirectional Live API for real-time audio/video.

tool-function-calling.comment: Documented function calling lets models request structured tool invocations the caller executes.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: Gemini function calling can emit multiple tool calls in a single turn for concurrent execution (parallel function calling).

structured-output.comment: Controlled generation enforces a caller-supplied response schema (responseSchema / structured output = server-enforced JSON Schema); a looser JSON output mode is also available.

system-prompt.comment: Distinct system instructions field supported; OpenAI-compatible endpoint uses the system/developer role.

image-input-vision.comment: Image understanding: Gemini accepts images as request input to reason over.

audio-input.comment: Audio understanding supported (speech input to Gemini), plus Chirp speech-to-text on the same platform.

document-pdf-input.comment: Native document/PDF understanding as a first-class input type.

image-generation.comment: Image generation via Imagen and native Gemini image generation on the same platform/account.

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: Speech generation from text (Gemini TTS / Chirp) available through the same platform.

embeddings-endpoint.comment: Text embeddings and multimodal embeddings endpoints available for retrieval/semantic search.

prompt-caching.comment: Context caching supported (both implicit/automatic and explicit caller-managed caches); cached input billed at ~10% of standard input cost, with a minimum around 2,048 tokens (checked 2026-07).

batch-api.comment: Asynchronous batch prediction / batch inference at 50% off standard on-demand token pricing.

max-context-window.comment: Largest reliably-confirmed window is 1,000,000 tokens (Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 3); the earlier Gemini 1.5 Pro offered a 2,000,000-token window. Windows vary by model and change often (checked 2026-07).

extended-reasoning.comment: Exposes 'thinking' / reasoning controls at the API level (thinking budget on Gemini reasoning models).

built-in-web-search.comment: First-party Grounding with Google Search tool callable via the API (also grounding with your own data / RAG Engine).

built-in-code-execution.comment: First-party code execution tool (sandboxed Python) callable via the API.

mcp-support.comment: Model Context Protocol supported through the Agent Development Kit / Agent Engine (connect agents to remote MCP-compatible tools) and the Cloud API Registry MCP server registry (checked 2026-07).

files-retrieval-api.comment: Managed retrieval/RAG via Vertex AI RAG Engine, Vector Search, and Vertex AI Search over ingested documents the model can reference.

agents-assistants-api.comment: Vertex AI Agent Engine provides a fully managed stateful agents runtime with session and short/long-term memory management above raw inference.

openai-compatible-api.comment: Documented OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions endpoint usable with the OpenAI SDK via a base-URL swap (…/endpoints/openapi), authenticating with a Google Cloud access token; works with Gemini and self-deployed models.

official-sdks.comment: First-party Google Gen AI SDK provides client libraries for Python, Node.js/TypeScript, Go, Java, and C#/.NET; broader Google Cloud client libraries also cover additional languages (checked 2026-07).

native-api-style.comment: Native primary style is the Google Cloud-native generateContent REST API; OpenAI Chat Completions and Anthropic Messages (for Claude) are additional compatibility shims.

webhooks-callbacks.comment: Async batch prediction runs as a long-running operation whose completion is discovered by polling or via Cloud Storage output, not classic HTTP webhooks (checked 2026-07).

published-sla.comment: Google publishes a Vertex AI Service Level Agreement (monthly uptime commitment with service-credit remedies); the exact percentage was not re-verified against the SLA document this pass (checked 2026-07).

rate-limit-model.comment: Default per-project/per-region quotas (dynamic shared quota, adjustable via quota requests) plus reserved capacity via Provisioned Throughput; a Priority pay-as-you-go tier is also offered.

provider-fallback-routing.comment: The global endpoint provides dynamic capacity routing and a Model Optimizer can auto-select among Gemini models, but there is no cross-provider failover gateway; not a defining gateway capability here (checked 2026-07).

status-page.comment: Covered by the public Google Cloud Service Health dashboard (status.cloud.google.com); no dedicated fetched source cited this pass, derived from the standard Google Cloud status page (checked 2026-07).

no-training-default.comment: Under Google's AI/ML Privacy Commitment, customer prompts/outputs on Vertex AI are not used to train Google's foundation models by default.

zero-data-retention.comment: Zero data retention / abbreviated data logging is available and configurable; note a default short-term (≈24h) caching for abuse monitoring that must be turned off at the project level to achieve true ZDR (checked 2026-07).

compliance-certifications.comment: Vertex AI inherits Google Cloud's portfolio: SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001 (and 27017/27018/27701/42001), HIPAA (BAA available, covered surface required), GDPR, FedRAMP High, and PCI DSS. Coverage is surface/model-specific; verify per model (checked 2026-07).

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: Google Cloud IAM plus Cloud Identity / Workforce Identity Federation (SSO/SAML) provide org-level access control and federation, alongside VPC Service Controls and CMEK.

pricing-model.comment: Primarily per-token pay-as-you-go (Standard and Priority PayGo); reserved capacity via Provisioned Throughput; batch at 50% off.

unified-billing.comment: A single Google Cloud billing account covers all models and vendors reachable through Vertex AI.

cache-batch-discount.comment: Batch inference at 50% off standard token pricing; context-cache reads billed at ~10% of standard input cost (≈90% discount).

cheapest-entry.comment: Lowest-friction start is the $300 Google Cloud free-trial credit, then self-serve pay-as-you-go per-token pricing.
